title: File-Based Routing
summary: File based routing turns your files and folders into the routes of your app.
You do not configure routes by hand.
The location of a file decides its URL.
Many modern frameworks use this idea.

sections.text: In many modern frameworks, you do not write out a list of routes. Instead, the way you arrange your files becomes the map of your site. This is called file based routing, and it keeps your structure and your URLs in sync.

sections.title: Structure Becomes Routes
sections.text: With file based routing, the folders and files you create directly define the URLs of your app. A file in an about folder becomes the about page. There is no separate routing setup to keep in sync, because the file system is the routing. Move a file, and its URL moves with it.

sections.title: Why It Is Nice
sections.text: This keeps things predictable. To find the code for a page, you look for the matching folder. To add a page, you add a file. New developers can guess where things are, because the structure mirrors the site. It is one less thing to configure and one less thing to get out of date.

media.alt: A file tree beside the matching site URLs it produces

sections.title: Rooms Numbered by Their Location
sections.text: File based routing is like a building where each room's number comes from where it physically is, floor two, third door. You never keep a separate list of room numbers, because the location is the number. Move a wall, and the numbering simply follows.

media.alt: Rooms whose numbers come directly from their location in the building

sections.title: How File Based Routing Works
items: You create folders and files for your pages.
Their location decides each page's URL.
There is no separate route list to maintain.
To add a page, add a file in the right place.
To find a page's code, follow its URL to the folder.

sections.text: This idea is so handy that it spread well beyond Next.js. Several other modern frameworks adopted file based routing because it removes a whole category of setup and mistakes.

terms.definition: Routes that come from your files and folders.
A directory that maps to part of a URL.
A page, placed to set its URL.
The address decided by a file's location.
The arrangement of files that becomes the routes.

questions.text: What does file based routing turn into routes?
questions.explanation: File based routing turns your files and folders into the app's routes.
A file's location in the structure decides its URL.

questions.text: Do you configure routes by hand with it?
questions.explanation: With file based routing you do not configure routes by hand, the file system does it.
